questions.question: Biology is defined as the science of:
questions.explanation: Biology is the science of life forms and living processes as stated in NCERT.

questions.question: Which of the following was deified by early man?
questions.explanation: Early man deified some inanimate matter like wind, sea and fire, and some among the animals and plants.

questions.question: The biggest spin-off of systematic description of life forms was:

options.B: Recognition of sharing of similarities among living organisms

questions.explanation: The biggest spin-off was the recognition of sharing of similarities among living organisms both horizontally and vertically.

questions.question: Cultural movements for conservation of biodiversity arose because of the realisation that:

options.A: All organisms are different from each other
options.B: All present-day living organisms are related to each other and to all organisms that ever lived on earth
options.C: Plants and animals are completely different
options.D: Humans are superior to all organisms

questions.explanation: The revelation that all present-day organisms are related to each other and to all organisms that ever lived humbled man and led to cultural movements for conservation of biodiversity.

questions.question: Which type of societies registered limited progress in biological knowledge?

options.B: Societies with an anthropocentric view of biology

questions.explanation: Societies which indulged in an anthropocentric view of biology could register limited progress in biological knowledge.

questions.question: The number of species known and described ranges between:
questions.explanation: The number of species that are known and described range between 1.7–1.8 million according to NCERT.

questions.question: Biodiversity refers to:

options.B: Number and types of organisms present on earth

questions.explanation: Biodiversity refers to the number and types of organisms present on earth.

questions.question: Each different kind of plant, animal or organism represents a:
questions.explanation: Each different kind of plant, animal or organism that you see represents a species.

questions.question: As we explore new areas, new organisms are:
questions.explanation: As we explore new areas, and even old ones, new organisms are continuously being identified.

questions.question: Local names of organisms vary from place to place because:

options.C: Local names are not standardised and differ regionally

questions.explanation: Local names vary from place to place, even within a country, creating confusion when communicating about organisms.

questions.question: Nomenclature is the process of:

options.B: Standardising the naming of living organisms

questions.explanation: Nomenclature is the process of standardising the naming of living organisms such that a particular organism is known by the same name all over the world.

questions.question: Nomenclature is only possible when:

options.B: The organism is correctly described and identified

questions.explanation: Nomenclature or naming is only possible when the organism is described correctly and we know to what organism the name is attached to — this is identification.

questions.question: Scientific names for plants are governed by:
questions.explanation: For plants, scientific names are based on agreed principles and criteria provided in International Code for Botanical Nomenclature (ICBN).

questions.question: Scientific names for animals are governed by:
questions.explanation: Animal taxonomists have evolved International Code of Zoological Nomenclature (ICZN) for naming animals.

questions.question: Scientific names ensure that each organism has:
questions.explanation: The scientific names ensure that each organism has only one name, acceptable to biologists all over the world.

questions.question: Each scientific name has how many components?
questions.explanation: Each scientific name has two components — the Generic name and the specific epithet.

questions.question: The system of providing a name with two components is called:
questions.explanation: The system of providing a name with two components (Generic name + specific epithet) is called Binomial nomenclature.

questions.question: Binomial nomenclature was given by:
questions.explanation: The binomial naming system was given by Carolus Linnaeus and is being practised by biologists all over the world.

questions.question: In the scientific name Mangifera indica, 'Mangifera' represents the:
questions.explanation: In Mangifera indica, 'Mangifera' represents the genus while 'indica' is the specific epithet.

questions.question: In the scientific name Mangifera indica, 'indica' represents the:
questions.explanation: In Mangifera indica, 'indica' is the specific epithet (species name).

questions.question: Biological names are generally written in which language?
questions.explanation: Biological names are generally in Latin and written in italics. They are Latinised or derived from Latin irrespective of their origin.

questions.question: When printed, biological names are written in:
questions.explanation: When printed, both words in a biological name are printed in italics to indicate their Latin origin.

questions.question: When handwritten, biological names are:
questions.explanation: When handwritten, both words in a biological name are separately underlined to indicate their Latin origin.

questions.question: In a biological name, the genus name starts with:
questions.explanation: The first word denoting the genus starts with a capital letter in binomial nomenclature.

questions.question: In a biological name, the specific epithet starts with:
questions.explanation: The specific epithet starts with a small letter as per the rules of binomial nomenclature.

questions.question: The scientific name of mango is correctly written as:
questions.explanation: The correct writing is Mangifera indica — genus starts with capital, specific epithet with small letter, both in italics.

questions.question: What does 'Linn.' written after Mangifera indica indicate?

options.B: The species was first described by Linnaeus

questions.explanation: Mangifera indica Linn. indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. The author's name appears in abbreviated form after the specific epithet.

questions.question: The author's name in a biological name is written:

options.C: After the specific epithet in abbreviated form

questions.explanation: The name of the author appears after the specific epithet at the end of the biological name and is written in an abbreviated form.

questions.question: Classification is the process of:

options.B: Grouping organisms into convenient categories based on observable characters

questions.explanation: Classification is the process by which anything is grouped into convenient categories based on some easily observable characters.

questions.question: The scientific term for the categories used to study organisms is:
questions.explanation: The scientific term for the convenient categories used to study organisms is taxa (singular: taxon).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a taxon?
questions.explanation: Habitat is not a taxon. Plants, animals, mammals, dogs etc. are all taxa at different levels.

questions.question: Taxonomy is the process of classification based on:

options.B: External, internal structure, cell structure, development process and ecological information

questions.explanation: External and internal structure, along with the structure of cell, development process and ecological information of organisms are essential and form the basis of modern taxonomic studies.

questions.question: The four processes basic to taxonomy are:
questions.explanation: Characterisation, identification, classification and nomenclature are the processes that are basic to taxonomy.

questions.question: The earliest classifications of organisms were based on:
questions.explanation: The earliest classifications were based on the 'uses' of various organisms for basic needs of food, clothing and shelter.

questions.question: Systematics is the branch of study concerned with:

options.B: Knowing different kinds of organisms and their diversities, and relationships among them

questions.explanation: Systematics was the branch referred to for knowing more about different kinds of organisms, their diversities and the relationships among them.

questions.question: The word 'systematics' is derived from the Latin word:
questions.explanation: The word systematics is derived from the Latin word 'systema' which means systematic arrangement of organisms.

questions.question: Linnaeus used which title for his publication on systematics?
questions.explanation: Linnaeus used 'Systema Naturae' as the title of his publication.

questions.question: Systematics takes into account which relationships between organisms?
questions.explanation: Systematics takes into account evolutionary relationships between organisms.

questions.question: Which of the following is an extraordinary habitat where living organisms are found?

options.C: Cold mountains, deciduous forests, oceans, fresh water lakes, deserts and hot springs

questions.explanation: Living organisms are found in extraordinary habitats including cold mountains, deciduous forests, oceans, fresh water lakes, deserts and hot springs.

questions.question: The question 'What is living?' has how many implicit questions?
questions.explanation: The question 'What is life?' has two implicit questions: one technical (what is living vs non-living) and one philosophical (what is the purpose of life).

questions.question: As scientists, which question about life do we NOT attempt to answer?

options.B: What is the purpose of life?
options.C: What makes living organisms different from non-living?
options.D: What are the characteristics of living organisms?

questions.explanation: As scientists, we shall not attempt answering the philosophical question of what the purpose of life is. We focus on the technical question of what is living.

questions.question: The scope of systematics was later enlarged to include:
questions.explanation: The scope of systematics was later enlarged to include identification, nomenclature and classification.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly defines 'identification' in taxonomy?

options.A: Giving a name to an organism
options.B: Knowing to what organism a name is attached after correct description

questions.explanation: Identification is knowing to what organism the name is attached to after the organism is correctly described.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about scientific names?

options.A: They are different in different countries
options.B: They are based on local languages
options.C: A description of any organism enables people anywhere in the world to arrive at the same name

questions.explanation: Description of any organism should enable the people in any part of the world to arrive at the same name — this is the purpose of scientific nomenclature.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes 'taxa'?

options.B: Convenient categories at various levels used to study organisms

questions.explanation: Taxa are convenient categories at different levels (species, genus, family, order, class, phylum, kingdom) used to study organisms.

questions.question: Which of the following represents taxa at DIFFERENT levels?
questions.explanation: Animals, mammals and dogs represent taxa at different levels — a dog is a mammal, and mammals are animals. They are at kingdom, class and species levels respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT TRUE about scientific nomenclature?

options.B: The same name has been used for many known organisms
options.C: Each organism has only one name

questions.explanation: Scientific names ensure that such a name has NOT been used for any other known organism — each organism has a unique name.

questions.question: Mangifera indica was first described by:
questions.explanation: Mangifera indica Linn. — the 'Linn.' indicates this species was first described by Linnaeus.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr is known as:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr, the Harvard University evolutionary biologist, has been called 'The Darwin of the 20th century'.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr was born on:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr was born on 5 July 1904, in Kempten, Germany.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr joined Harvard's Faculty of Arts and Sciences in:
questions.explanation: Mayr joined Harvard's Faculty of Arts and Sciences in 1953.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr retired from Harvard in:
questions.explanation: Mayr joined Harvard in 1953 and retired in 1975, assuming the title Alexander Agassiz Professor of Zoology Emeritus.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr's title at retirement was:

options.B: Alexander Agassiz Professor of Zoology Emeritus

questions.explanation: Mayr retired assuming the title Alexander Agassiz Professor of Zoology Emeritus.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr's research spanned which fields?

options.B: Ornithology, taxonomy, zoogeography, evolution, systematics, and history and philosophy of biology

questions.explanation: Throughout his nearly 80-year career, Mayr's research spanned ornithology, taxonomy, zoogeography, evolution, systematics, and the history and philosophy of biology.

questions.question: What did Ernst Mayr make the central question of evolutionary biology?
questions.explanation: Mayr almost single-handedly made the origin of species diversity the central question of evolutionary biology that it is today.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr pioneered the currently accepted definition of a:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr also pioneered the currently accepted definition of a biological species.

questions.question: The 'triple crown' of biology awarded to Ernst Mayr consists of:

options.A: Nobel Prize, Darwin Award, Mayr Prize
options.B: Balzan Prize, International Prize for Biology, Crafoord Prize
options.C: Fields Medal, Darwin Prize, Zoology Award
options.D: Darwin Medal, Mendel Prize, Linnaeus Award

questions.explanation: Mayr was awarded the Balzan Prize (1983), the International Prize for Biology (1994), and the Crafoord Prize (1999) — regarded as the triple crown of biology.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr received the Balzan Prize in:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr was awarded the Balzan Prize in 1983.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr received the International Prize for Biology in:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr was awarded the International Prize for Biology in 1994.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr received the Crafoord Prize in:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr was awarded the Crafoord Prize in 1999.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr died at the age of:
questions.explanation: Mayr died at the age of 100 in the year 2004.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr died in the year:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr died at the age of 100 in the year 2004.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr was born in which country?
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr was born on 5 July 1904 in Kempten, Germany.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr was associated with which university?
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr was associated with Harvard University as an evolutionary biologist.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as an extraordinary habitat for living organisms in NCERT?
questions.explanation: NCERT lists cold mountains, deciduous forests, oceans, fresh water lakes, deserts and hot springs as extraordinary habitats. Volcanoes are not mentioned.

questions.question: Which of the following creates a need for standardised naming of organisms?

options.A: Organisms are too small to study
options.B: Local names vary from place to place creating confusion
options.C: Scientific names are easier to pronounce

questions.explanation: Local names vary from place to place, even within a country, which creates confusion. Hence, standardisation through nomenclature is needed.

questions.question: The first word in a biological name represents the:
questions.explanation: The first word in a biological name represents the genus while the second component denotes the specific epithet.

questions.question: The second word in a biological name represents the:
questions.explanation: The second component in a biological name denotes the specific epithet (species name).

questions.question: Which of the following pairs correctly shows genus and specific epithet?
questions.explanation: Mangifera is the genus (capital first letter) and indica is the specific epithet (small first letter).

questions.question: Which of the following statements about binomial nomenclature is INCORRECT?

options.B: It was given by Carolus Linnaeus
options.C: The genus name starts with a small letter
options.D: Both words are in Latin or Latinised

questions.explanation: The genus name starts with a CAPITAL letter, not a small letter. The specific epithet starts with a small letter.

questions.question: The study of taxonomy is useful in which of the following areas?

options.B: Agriculture, forestry, industry and knowing bio-resources and their diversity

questions.explanation: Taxonomic studies of various species of plants and animals are useful in agriculture, forestry, industry and in general for knowing our bio-resources and their diversity.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the basic processes of taxonomy?
questions.explanation: The four basic processes of taxonomy are characterisation, identification, classification and nomenclature. Mutation is a genetic process, not a taxonomic one.

questions.question: The number of species remaining unknown (not yet identified) is:

options.B: A large number still remains unknown

questions.explanation: Millions of plants and animals have been identified and described but a large number still remains unknown.

questions.question: ICBN stands for:
questions.explanation: ICBN stands for International Code for Botanical Nomenclature, which governs scientific names for plants.

questions.question: ICZN stands for:

options.D: International Code of Zoology and Nomenclature

questions.explanation: ICZN stands for International Code of Zoological Nomenclature, which governs scientific names for animals.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines a 'species' in taxonomy?

options.A: A group of organisms living in the same area
options.B: A group of individual organisms with fundamental similarities
options.C: A group of organisms with the same habitat
options.D: All organisms of the same size

questions.explanation: Taxonomic studies consider a group of individual organisms with fundamental similarities as a species.

questions.question: One species can be distinguished from another closely related species based on:
questions.explanation: One should be able to distinguish one species from the other closely related species based on the distinct morphological differences.

questions.question: Panthera leo is the scientific name of:
questions.explanation: Panthera leo is the scientific name for lion.

questions.question: Solanum tuberosum is the scientific name of:
questions.explanation: Solanum tuberosum is the scientific name for potato.

questions.question: The specific epithet for lion (Panthera leo) is:
questions.explanation: In Panthera leo, 'leo' is the specific epithet representing the lion species.

questions.question: The specific epithet for potato (Solanum tuberosum) is:
questions.explanation: In Solanum tuberosum, 'tuberosum' is the specific epithet for potato.

questions.question: Which genus has the specific epithet 'tigris'?
questions.explanation: Panthera tigris is the scientific name for tiger — Panthera is the genus and tigris is the specific epithet.

questions.question: Genus Solanum includes which of the following species?
questions.explanation: Genus Solanum includes species like nigrum and melongena (brinjal) and tuberosum (potato).

questions.question: The scientific name for human being is:
questions.explanation: Human beings belong to the species sapiens which is grouped in the genus Homo. The scientific name is Homo sapiens.

questions.question: Genus Panthera differs from genus Felis. What does genus Felis include?
questions.explanation: Genus Felis includes cats, while Genus Panthera includes lions, tigers and leopards.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly pairs genus and its members?

options.D: Genus Panthera: lion, leopard and tiger

questions.explanation: Genus Panthera includes lion (P. leo), leopard (P. pardus) and tiger (P. tigris).

questions.question: Genus comprises a group of related species which has:

options.B: Fewer characters in common than species of other genera
options.C: More characters in common compared to species of other genera

questions.explanation: Genus comprises a group of related species which has more characters in common in comparison to species of other genera.

questions.question: Genera are defined as:
questions.explanation: We can say that genera are aggregates of closely related species.

questions.question: Potato and brinjal are two different species but both belong to the genus:
questions.explanation: Potato (Solanum tuberosum) and brinjal (Solanum melongena) are two different species but both belong to the genus Solanum.

questions.question: The scientific name of brinjal is:
questions.explanation: The scientific name of brinjal is Solanum melongena.

questions.question: Family is characterised on the basis of:

options.A: Only vegetative features of plant species
options.B: Only reproductive features of plant species
options.C: Both vegetative and reproductive features of plant species

questions.explanation: Families are characterised on the basis of both vegetative and reproductive features of plant species.

questions.question: Which plant family includes genera Solanum, Petunia and Datura?
questions.explanation: Three different genera — Solanum, Petunia and Datura — are placed in the family Solanaceae.

questions.question: Which animal family includes genus Panthera (lion, tiger, leopard) and genus Felis (cats)?
questions.explanation: Genus Panthera (lion, tiger, leopard) and genus Felis (cats) are placed in the family Felidae.

questions.question: Cats and dogs are separated into which two different families respectively?
questions.explanation: Cats belong to family Felidae and dogs belong to family Canidae — they are separated into two different families.

questions.question: Family has a group of related genera with ______ number of similarities as compared to genus and species:
questions.explanation: Family has a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species.

questions.question: Order being a higher category is the assemblage of:

options.C: Families which exhibit a few similar characters

questions.explanation: Order being a higher category, is the assemblage of families which exhibit a few similar characters.

questions.question: Plant families Convolvulaceae and Solanaceae are included in the order:
questions.explanation: Plant families Convolvulaceae and Solanaceae are included in the order Polymoniales mainly based on the floral characters.

questions.question: The animal order Carnivora includes which families?
questions.explanation: The animal order Carnivora includes families like Felidae and Canidae.

questions.question: Order Primata includes which animals?
questions.explanation: Order Primata comprises monkey, gorilla and gibbon.

questions.question: Class Mammalia includes which orders?
questions.explanation: Order Primata and order Carnivora are both placed in class Mammalia, along with other orders.

questions.question: Class includes:
questions.explanation: Class includes related orders.

questions.question: Order Carnivora includes animals like:
questions.explanation: Order Carnivora includes animals like tiger, cat and dog.

questions.question: Phylum groups together organisms having which common features?

options.C: Common features like presence of notochord and dorsal hollow neural system

questions.explanation: Animals like fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals, based on common features like presence of notochord and dorsal hollow neural system, are included in phylum Chordata.

questions.question: What is the equivalent of Phylum in plants?
questions.explanation: In case of plants, classes with a few similar characters are assigned to a higher category called Division (equivalent to Phylum).

questions.question: Phylum Chordata includes which of the following groups?

options.B: Fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals

questions.explanation: Classes comprising animals like fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds along with mammals constitute phylum Chordata.

questions.question: Kingdom Animalia includes:

options.B: All animals belonging to various phyla

questions.explanation: All animals belonging to various phyla are assigned to the highest category called Kingdom Animalia.

questions.question: Kingdom Plantae comprises:
questions.explanation: Kingdom Plantae is distinct and comprises all plants from various divisions.

questions.question: Kingdom is the ______ category in the taxonomic hierarchy:
questions.explanation: All animals are assigned to the highest category called Kingdom Animalia. Kingdom is the highest category.

questions.question: Species is the ______ category in the taxonomic hierarchy:
questions.explanation: All organisms, including those in the plant and animal kingdoms, have species as the lowest category.

questions.question: As we go higher from species to kingdom, the number of common characteristics:
questions.explanation: As we go higher from species to kingdom, the number of common characteristics goes on decreasing.

questions.question: Lower the taxa, ______ are the characteristics that the members within the taxon share:
questions.explanation: Lower the taxa, more are the characteristics that the members within the taxon share.

questions.question: Higher the category, ______ is the difficulty of determining the relationship to other taxa at the same level:
questions.explanation: Higher the category, greater is the difficulty of determining the relationship to other taxa at the same level. Hence, the problem of classification becomes more complex.

questions.question: The correct ascending order of taxonomic categories from lowest to highest is:

options.A: Kingdom → Phylum → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species
options.B: Species → Genus → Family → Order → Class → Phylum → Kingdom
options.C: Species → Family → Genus → Order → Class → Phylum → Kingdom
options.D: Genus → Species → Family → Order → Class → Kingdom → Phylum

questions.explanation: The correct ascending order is Species → Genus → Family → Order → Class → Phylum/Division → Kingdom.

questions.question: The correct descending order of taxonomic categories from highest to lowest is:

options.A: Species → Genus → Family → Order → Class → Phylum → Kingdom
options.B: Kingdom → Phylum → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species
options.C: Kingdom → Class → Phylum → Order → Family → Genus → Species
options.D: Kingdom → Order → Phylum → Class → Family → Genus → Species

questions.explanation: The correct descending order is Kingdom → Phylum/Division → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species.

questions.question: The taxonomic category just above species is:
questions.explanation: The hierarchy is: Species → Genus → Family → Order → Class → Phylum → Kingdom. Genus is just above Species.

questions.question: The taxonomic category just below family is:
questions.explanation: In the hierarchy Species → Genus → Family, Genus is just below Family.

questions.question: The taxonomic category between Order and Phylum is:
questions.explanation: The order is: Order → Class → Phylum. Class comes between Order and Phylum.

questions.question: The taxonomic category just below Kingdom is:
questions.explanation: Kingdom → Phylum/Division is the order. Phylum/Division is just below Kingdom.

questions.question: The biological name of housefly is:
questions.explanation: The biological name of housefly is Musca domestica — Musca (genus, capital) and domestica (specific epithet, small letter), written in italics.

questions.question: The biological name of wheat is:
questions.explanation: The biological name of wheat is Triticum aestivum.

questions.question: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to which class?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to class Insecta.

questions.question: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to which order?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to order Diptera.

questions.question: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to which family?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to family Muscidae.

questions.question: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to which phylum?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to phylum Arthropoda.

questions.question: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to which family?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to family Hominidae.

questions.question: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to which order?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to order Primata.

questions.question: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to which class?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to class Mammalia.

questions.question: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to which phylum?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to phylum Chordata.

questions.question: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to which family?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to family Anacardiaceae.

questions.question: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to which order?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to order Sapindales.

questions.question: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to which class?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to class Dicotyledonae.

questions.question: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to which division?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to division Angiospermae.

questions.question: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to which family?
questions.explanation: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to family Poaceae.

questions.question: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to which order?
questions.explanation: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to order Poales.

questions.question: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to which class?
questions.explanation: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to class Monocotyledonae.

questions.question: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to which division?
questions.explanation: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to division Angiospermae.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of organisms belong to the same genus?
questions.explanation: Potato (Solanum tuberosum) and brinjal (Solanum melongena) both belong to genus Solanum.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of organisms belong to the same family?
questions.explanation: Lion (Panthera leo, family Felidae) and cat (Felis, family Felidae) belong to the same family Felidae.

questions.question: The concept of 'taxon' was introduced because:

options.B: There is a need for ranks and categories to systematically classify organisms
options.C: All names should be in Latin
options.D: Scientists prefer to work with ranks

questions.explanation: Taxon represents a unit of classification. Categories at various levels are needed to systematically classify and study organisms.

questions.question: Taxonomic categories are distinct biological entities and NOT merely:
questions.explanation: These taxonomic groups/categories are distinct biological entities and not merely morphological aggregates.

questions.question: Insects represent a group sharing common features like:
questions.explanation: Insects represent a group of organisms sharing common features like three pairs of jointed legs.

questions.question: The basic requirement to place an organism in various categories is:

options.B: Knowledge of characters of an individual or group

questions.explanation: The basic requirement is the knowledge of characters of an individual or group of organisms. This helps in identifying similarities and dissimilarities.

questions.question: Sub-categories in the taxonomic hierarchy are developed to facilitate:

options.B: More sound and scientific placement of various taxa

questions.explanation: Taxonomists have also developed sub-categories in this hierarchy to facilitate more sound and scientific placement of various taxa.

questions.question: The genus of housefly is:
questions.explanation: The genus of housefly is Musca (Musca domestica).

questions.question: The genus of man is:
questions.explanation: The genus of man is Homo (Homo sapiens).

questions.question: The genus of mango is:
questions.explanation: The genus of mango is Mangifera (Mangifera indica).

questions.question: The genus of wheat is:
questions.explanation: The genus of wheat is Triticum (Triticum aestivum).

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct scientific name for tiger?
questions.explanation: The scientific name of tiger is Panthera tigris.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct scientific name for leopard?
questions.explanation: The scientific name of leopard is Panthera pardus.

questions.question: What is the specific epithet of leopard?
questions.explanation: The specific epithet of leopard is pardus (Panthera pardus).

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about Phylum Chordata?

options.C: Fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals — all belong to it

questions.explanation: Fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds along with mammals — all are included in phylum Chordata based on common features like notochord and dorsal hollow neural system.

questions.question: The two key common features that unite all members of phylum Chordata are:

options.B: Presence of notochord and dorsal hollow neural system

questions.explanation: All members of phylum Chordata share the common features of presence of notochord and dorsal hollow neural system.

questions.question: Classification is NOT a single step process because:

options.B: It involves hierarchy of steps where each step represents a rank or category

questions.explanation: Classification is not a single step process but involves hierarchy of steps in which each step represents a rank or category.

questions.question: A 'taxon' represents:

options.B: A unit of classification at any level

questions.explanation: Each rank or taxon, in fact, represents a unit of classification.

questions.question: All categories together constitute the:
questions.explanation: All categories together constitute the taxonomic hierarchy.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents the common taxonomic categories from lowest to highest?

options.A: Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
options.B: Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom
options.C: Species, Family, Genus, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom
options.D: Genus, Species, Family, Order, Kingdom, Phylum, Class

questions.explanation: The common categories from lowest to highest are: Species → Genus → Family → Order → Class → Phylum or Division (for plants) → Kingdom.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a common taxonomic category?
questions.explanation: Ecosystem is an ecological concept, not a taxonomic category. Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus and Species are all taxonomic categories.

questions.question: Mango and wheat both belong to which division?
questions.explanation: Both mango (Mangifera indica) and wheat (Triticum aestivum) belong to division Angiospermae.

questions.question: Mango and wheat differ in their class because:

options.A: Mango is Monocot and wheat is Dicot
options.B: Mango is Dicot (Dicotyledonae) and wheat is Monocot (Monocotyledonae)

questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to class Dicotyledonae while wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to class Monocotyledonae.

questions.question: Man and housefly belong to the same:
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) and housefly (Musca domestica) both belong to Kingdom Animalia, but they differ in phylum (Chordata vs Arthropoda), class and all other categories.

questions.question: Why are classification systems changing every now and then?

options.A: Scientists get bored of old systems
options.B: New organisms are continuously being discovered and new scientific data revises our understanding

questions.explanation: As we explore new areas, new organisms are continuously being identified. New scientific data (molecular, genetic) also revises our understanding of evolutionary relationships, causing classification systems to change.

questions.question: What do we learn from identification of individuals and populations?

options.B: Similarities and dissimilarities among organisms which helps in placing them in appropriate taxa
options.C: Only the economic uses of organisms

questions.explanation: Identification helps in recognising similarities and dissimilarities among individuals of the same kind as well as other kinds, enabling their placement in appropriate taxonomic categories.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correctly written scientific name of mango from the NCERT exercise?
questions.explanation: Mangifera indica is the correctly written name — genus with capital M, specific epithet with small i, both in italics.

questions.question: In Mangifera Indica (wrong writing), what is the error?

options.B: The specific epithet 'Indica' should start with a small letter 'i'
options.C: The genus name should start with small letter
options.D: The name should not be italicised

questions.explanation: The specific epithet should always start with a small letter. 'Indica' should be written as 'indica'. The correct name is Mangifera indica.

questions.question: Which of the following sequences of taxonomical categories is CORRECT?
questions.explanation: The correct sequence from NCERT exercise is: Species → Genus → Order → Phylum (skipping family and class).

questions.question: Which of the following is a phylogenetic approach in taxonomy?

options.A: Classifying organisms only by their colour
options.B: Classifying organisms based on evolutionary relationships
options.C: Classifying organisms only by their habitat
options.D: Classifying organisms by their economic importance

questions.explanation: Systematics takes into account evolutionary relationships between organisms — this is the phylogenetic or evolutionary approach.

questions.question: Order Primata and Order Carnivora are both placed in class:
questions.explanation: Order Primata (monkey, gorilla, gibbon) and Order Carnivora (tiger, cat, dog) are both placed in class Mammalia.

questions.question: The term 'taxon' in plural is written as:
questions.explanation: The scientific term is taxon (singular) and taxa (plural).

questions.question: Which animal class includes fishes, amphibians and reptiles along with mammals and birds?

options.A: These all belong to different phyla
options.B: They all belong to different classes within phylum Chordata
options.C: They all are in class Mammalia
options.D: They all are in phylum Arthropoda

questions.explanation: Fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals are all in phylum Chordata but belong to different classes within it.

questions.question: Which of the following does NOT belong to the order Primata?
questions.explanation: Tiger belongs to order Carnivora (family Felidae), not order Primata. Monkey, gorilla and gibbon are in order Primata.

questions.question: The scientific name of potato is:
questions.explanation: The scientific name of potato is Solanum tuberosum.

questions.question: Which of the following belongs to genus Mangifera?
questions.explanation: Mango belongs to genus Mangifera (Mangifera indica).

questions.question: Which of the following belongs to genus Triticum?
questions.explanation: Wheat belongs to genus Triticum (Triticum aestivum).

questions.question: Which of the following organisms belongs to phylum Arthropoda?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to phylum Arthropoda.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms belongs to phylum Chordata?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to phylum Chordata.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about taxonomy is INCORRECT?

options.B: It was always based on evolutionary relationships
options.C: The earliest classifications were based on uses of organisms
options.D: Modern taxonomy uses cell structure and developmental information

questions.explanation: Taxonomy was NOT always based on evolutionary relationships. The earliest classifications were based on uses of organisms (food, clothing, shelter). Evolutionary relationships were considered later through systematics.

questions.question: According to NCERT, identifying similarities and dissimilarities helps in:
questions.explanation: Identifying similarities and dissimilarities among individuals of the same kind as well as of other kinds helps in placing them in appropriate taxonomic categories.

questions.question: The three genera Solanum, Petunia and Datura are all placed in which plant family?
questions.explanation: Three different genera — Solanum, Petunia and Datura — are all placed in the family Solanaceae.

questions.question: The order Polymoniales is mainly based on which characters?
questions.explanation: Plant families like Convolvulaceae and Solanaceae are included in the order Polymoniales mainly based on the floral characters.

questions.question: Leopard belongs to which genus?
questions.explanation: Leopard (Panthera pardus) belongs to genus Panthera.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms is NOT in order Carnivora?
questions.explanation: Monkey belongs to order Primata. Tiger, cat and dog are all in order Carnivora.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of organisms belong to the same order?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) and monkey both belong to order Primata.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of organisms belong to the same class?
questions.explanation: Man and tiger both belong to class Mammalia. Housefly is in class Insecta, fish are in a different class, lizard in Reptilia.

questions.question: A singular unit of classification at any taxonomic level is called:
questions.explanation: A taxon represents a single unit of classification at any level in the taxonomic hierarchy.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation 'Linn.' represent in 'Mangifera indica Linn.'?

options.B: Linnaeus — the author who first described the species
options.C: Latin — showing it is in Latin

questions.explanation: 'Linn.' is the abbreviated form of Linnaeus, indicating that this species (Mangifera indica) was first described by Carolus Linnaeus.

questions.question: Gorilla belongs to which order?
questions.explanation: Gorilla belongs to order Primata along with monkey and gibbon.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct example of binomial nomenclature?
questions.explanation: Homo sapiens — genus 'Homo' with capital H and specific epithet 'sapiens' with small s — this is the correctly written binomial name.

questions.question: Two members of the same species will always belong to the same:

options.B: Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum and Kingdom (all levels)

questions.explanation: Members of the same species belong to all the same taxonomic levels — same genus, family, order, class, phylum and kingdom.

questions.question: Two members of the same genus but different species will belong to the same:

options.C: Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum and Kingdom (but not species)

questions.explanation: Members of the same genus but different species share the same genus, family, order, class, phylum and kingdom, but differ at the species level.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between lion and tiger?
questions.explanation: Lion (Panthera leo) and tiger (Panthera tigris) are of the same genus (Panthera) but different species.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between cat and dog?

options.C: Same order (Carnivora), different families (Felidae vs Canidae)
options.D: Same phylum, same class, same order, same family

questions.explanation: Cat (family Felidae) and dog (family Canidae) belong to the same order (Carnivora) but to different families.

questions.question: The biological names are Latinised or derived from Latin irrespective of:
questions.explanation: Biological names are Latinised or derived from Latin irrespective of their origin — they may come from any language but are always Latinised.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of binomial nomenclature?

options.B: Words are in Latin or Latinised
options.C: It was given by Carolus Linnaeus
options.D: The specific epithet starts with a capital letter

questions.explanation: The specific epithet starts with a SMALL letter, not a capital letter. Only the genus name starts with a capital letter.

questions.question: Taxonomical studies have led to the development of common categories. Which category is NOT among them?
questions.explanation: The common taxonomic categories are kingdom, phylum or division (for plants), class, order, family, genus and species. Ecosystem is an ecological concept, not a taxonomic category.

questions.question: The science of classification is called:
questions.explanation: The process of classification is taxonomy. Taxonomy uses external and internal structure, cell structure, development process and ecological information.

questions.question: The taxonomic hierarchy shown in Figure 1.1 of NCERT shows categories in:
questions.explanation: The taxonomic categories from species to kingdom are shown in ascending order starting with species in Figure 1.1 of NCERT.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about the taxonomic category 'Order'?

options.A: It has the same number of similar characters as genus
options.B: It has fewer similar characters than those that distinguish different genera in a family
options.C: It has more similar characters than species
options.D: It is below family in the hierarchy

questions.explanation: The similar characters in an order are less in number as compared to different genera included in a family.

questions.question: Human beings being classified into order Primata indicates that humans share common features with:
questions.explanation: Order Primata includes monkey, gorilla, gibbon and man — all belong to order Primata indicating shared features.

questions.question: The unit kebo101.pdf uploaded represents NCERT textbook for which class?
questions.explanation: The NCERT Biology textbook (kebo101) is the Class 11 Biology textbook, Chapter 1 — The Living World.

questions.question: Which of the following is the FULL form of the common taxonomic hierarchy in correct order?

options.A: King Philip Came Over For Good Soup
options.B: Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
options.C: Both A and B (A is the mnemonic for B)
options.D: Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Group, Species

questions.explanation: Kingdom → Phylum/Division → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species is the standard hierarchy. 'King Philip Came Over For Good Soup' is a common mnemonic for it.

questions.question: The common name 'Alsatian' refers to a breed of dog. In taxonomy, this would represent which level?
questions.explanation: NCERT uses the example — 'if we think of Alsatians, we know what we are talking about' — indicating Alsatian represents a specific type/breed at species or sub-species level.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about the description of living organisms?

options.A: It began very early in human history
options.B: It began much later in human history

questions.explanation: The description of living organisms including human beings began much later in human history.

questions.question: Both mango and wheat are Angiosperms. They differ at the level of:
questions.explanation: Both are in division Angiospermae but differ at the class level — mango is Dicotyledonae and wheat is Monocotyledonae.

questions.question: Man and housefly first differ (share different taxa) at the level of:
questions.explanation: Man (phylum Chordata) and housefly (phylum Arthropoda) first differ at the phylum level, though both are in Kingdom Animalia.

questions.question: Man and lion first differ (share different taxa) at the level of:
questions.explanation: Man (order Primata) and lion (order Carnivora) first differ at the order level. They share kingdom (Animalia), phylum (Chordata) and class (Mammalia).

questions.question: Which of the following pair shares the MOST taxonomic categories in common?
questions.explanation: Man and monkey share kingdom, phylum, class and order (Primata) — 4+ common categories. Man and lion share 3 (kingdom, phylum, class). Man and tiger also share 3. Man and housefly share only 1 (kingdom).

questions.question: The ecological conflict and cooperation among members of a population and among populations of a community, or even the molecular traffic inside a cell — all make us reflect on:
questions.explanation: The ecological conflict, cooperation and molecular traffic inside a cell all make us deeply reflect on — what indeed is life?

questions.question: Which of the following is a PHILOSOPHICAL question about life?

options.A: What is living as opposed to non-living?
options.B: What are the characteristics of living organisms?
options.C: What is the purpose of life?

questions.explanation: The philosophical question about life seeks answer to what the purpose of life is. Scientists do not attempt to answer this question.

questions.question: Which of the following is a TECHNICAL question about life?

options.A: What is the purpose of life?
options.C: What is living as opposed to non-living?
options.D: What is the meaning of existence?

questions.explanation: The technical question seeks answer to what living is as opposed to the non-living. This is the scientific question we try to answer.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an aspect included in the scope of systematics after its enlargement?
questions.explanation: The scope of systematics was enlarged to include identification, nomenclature and classification — genetic engineering is not part of systematics.

questions.question: Scientific names ensure a name has NOT been used for any other known organism. This property is called:
questions.explanation: Scientific names ensure uniqueness — that the same name has not been used for any other known organism.

questions.question: The word 'biodiversity' refers to:

options.B: Number and types of organisms present on earth
options.D: Genetic diversity within a species only

questions.explanation: Biodiversity refers to the number and types of organisms present on earth.

questions.question: Which two kingdoms are specifically mentioned as animal and plant kingdoms in NCERT Chapter 1?
questions.explanation: NCERT states 'Henceforth, we will refer to these two groups as animal and plant kingdoms' — referring to Kingdom Animalia and Kingdom Plantae.

questions.question: The number of species on earth that still remains unknown is described as:
questions.explanation: Millions of plants and animals have been identified and described but a large number still remains unknown.

questions.question: Which of the following examples of classification is given in NCERT related to 'Wheat'?

options.A: When you think of Wheat, you picture all cereals
options.B: When you try to talk of Wheat, the picture in each mind will be of wheat plants, not of rice or any other plant
options.D: Wheat and rice are the same taxa

questions.explanation: NCERT gives the example: 'if we try to talk of Wheat, the picture in each of our minds will be of wheat plants, not of rice or any other plant' — demonstrating specific classification.

questions.question: Which of the following does NCERT use as an example to explain taxa at different levels?
questions.explanation: NCERT uses 'animals', 'mammals' and 'dogs' as examples to illustrate taxa at different levels — a dog is a mammal and mammals are animals.

questions.question: The term 'taxonomic category' refers to:

options.A: A category that is not part of taxonomy
options.B: A category that is part of overall taxonomic arrangement

questions.explanation: Since the category is a part of overall taxonomic arrangement, it is called the taxonomic category.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'taxonomic hierarchy' mean?

options.B: All categories together at different levels forming a hierarchical system

questions.explanation: All categories together constitute the taxonomic hierarchy — it is the system of all ranks from species to kingdom arranged in order.

questions.question: According to NCERT, when we use the term 'mammals', we think of animals with:
questions.explanation: NCERT says 'if we were to say mammals, you would, of course, think of animals with external ears and body hair.'

questions.question: The scientific name ensures the same organism is known by:

options.B: The same name all over the world

questions.explanation: Standardisation through nomenclature ensures that a particular organism is known by the same name all over the world.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents classification hierarchy from broader to more specific?

options.B: Kingdom → Phylum → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species
options.C: Kingdom → Class → Phylum → Order → Family → Genus → Species
options.D: Species → Genus → Family → Order → Class → Kingdom → Phylum

questions.explanation: The correct order from broadest to most specific is Kingdom → Phylum/Division → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species.

questions.question: The earliest human interest in biology was driven by the need to:

options.B: Find sources for basic needs of food, clothing and shelter

questions.explanation: In early days, human beings needed to find sources for their basic needs of food, clothing and shelter. Hence, the earliest classifications were based on the 'uses' of various organisms.

questions.question: What common characteristic do all members of phylum Arthropoda share?

options.A: Notochord and dorsal hollow neural system

questions.explanation: While NCERT specifically mentions Arthropoda in the table for housefly, Arthropoda members share jointed appendages. Insects (class in Arthropoda) have three pairs of jointed legs specifically.

questions.question: Family Hominidae belongs to which order?
questions.explanation: Family Hominidae (which includes Man — Homo sapiens) belongs to order Primata.

questions.question: Which organism given in NCERT Table 1.1 belongs to class Insecta?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to class Insecta as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: Which organism given in NCERT Table 1.1 belongs to order Sapindales?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to order Sapindales as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: Which organism given in NCERT Table 1.1 belongs to family Poaceae?
questions.explanation: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to family Poaceae as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: Ernst Mayr's career spanned approximately how many years?
questions.explanation: NCERT mentions 'Throughout his nearly 80-year career' referring to Ernst Mayr's research career.

questions.question: Which of the following is the MOST inclusive (broadest) taxon?
questions.explanation: Kingdom is the highest and most inclusive (broadest) taxon in the taxonomic hierarchy.

questions.question: Which of the following is the LEAST inclusive (most specific) taxon?
questions.explanation: Species is the lowest and least inclusive (most specific) taxon — all organisms have species as the lowest category.

questions.question: In the taxonomic classification of Man, which is the CORRECT family?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to family Hominidae.

questions.question: Which of the following genera are placed in family Solanaceae?
questions.explanation: Three different genera — Solanum, Petunia and Datura — are placed in the family Solanaceae.

questions.question: What is the specific epithet of the housefly?
questions.explanation: In Musca domestica, 'domestica' (small d) is the specific epithet of the housefly.

questions.question: What is the specific epithet of wheat?
questions.explanation: In Triticum aestivum, 'aestivum' (small a) is the specific epithet of wheat.

questions.question: The classification of organisms is described in NCERT as necessary because:

options.A: We have too many names for organisms
options.B: It is nearly impossible to study all living organisms without grouping them into categories

questions.explanation: Since it is nearly impossible to study all the living organisms, it is necessary to devise some means — classification — to make this possible.

questions.question: The scientific name of a species serves as identification because it:

options.B: Enables people anywhere in the world to arrive at the same name from the organism's description
options.C: Is short and easy to remember
options.D: Is based on colour and size

questions.explanation: Description of any organism should enable people in any part of the world to arrive at the same name — making it a universal identifier.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Ernst Mayr is INCORRECT?

options.B: He was associated with Harvard University
options.C: He received the Nobel Prize for Biology
options.D: He pioneered the currently accepted definition of a biological species

questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr received the Balzan Prize, International Prize for Biology and Crafoord Prize. He did NOT receive the Nobel Prize. Nobel Prize is not given for biology in general.

questions.question: Mango and wheat first differ (share different taxa) at the level of:
questions.explanation: Mango and wheat both belong to kingdom Plantae and division Angiospermae, but first differ at the class level — mango is Dicotyledonae, wheat is Monocotyledonae.

questions.question: Mango (Mangifera indica) and wheat (Triticum aestivum) share which taxonomic categories?
questions.explanation: Mango and wheat both belong to Kingdom Plantae and Division Angiospermae, but differ at class (Dicot vs Monocot), order, family, genus and species.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct statement from NCERT about genus?

options.A: Genus is the lowest taxonomic category
options.B: A genus may have one or more than one specific epithets representing different organisms but having morphological similarities
options.D: Genus is the highest taxonomic category

questions.explanation: Each genus may have one or more than one specific epithets representing different organisms, but having morphological similarities.

questions.question: Family Anacardiaceae to which mango belongs contains which genus?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to genus Mangifera and family Anacardiaceae.

questions.question: The specific epithet of mango (Mangifera indica) 'indica' indicates:

options.B: The plant is an indicator species
options.C: It is the specific name for this particular species of Mangifera
options.D: The plant grows only in India

questions.explanation: 'indica' is the specific epithet — the particular species name within the genus Mangifera. While it is often associated with India (origin), taxonomically it simply designates the species.

questions.question: The process by which an organism is correctly described so that its name can be attached is called:
questions.explanation: Identification is the process of correctly describing the organism and knowing to what organism the name is attached.

questions.question: According to NCERT, which of the following is the main reason why local names fail as a system of communication?

options.A: They are too difficult to learn
options.B: They vary from place to place, even within a country, creating confusion
options.C: They are not written in Latin

questions.explanation: Local names vary from place to place, even within a country, creating confusion when communicating about specific organisms.

questions.question: The concept that all present-day living organisms are related to each other is associated with which cultural movement?
questions.explanation: The revelation that all present-day organisms are related to each other and to all organisms that ever lived humbled man and led to cultural movements for conservation of biodiversity.

questions.question: The title 'The Darwin of the 20th century' was given to:
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr, the Harvard University evolutionary biologist, has been called 'The Darwin of the 20th century'.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a field of research that Ernst Mayr worked in?
questions.explanation: NCERT mentions Mayr's research spanned ornithology, taxonomy, zoogeography, evolution, systematics, and history and philosophy of biology. Genetics and molecular biology are not mentioned.

questions.question: In which city was Ernst Mayr born?
questions.explanation: Ernst Mayr was born on 5 July 1904 in Kempten, Germany.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms are shown in the NCERT chapter's introductory page to represent the living world?
questions.explanation: The introductory page of the NCERT Unit 1 shows an illustration of a seahorse representing the diversity in the living world.

questions.question: In systematic studies, which is the PRIMARY concern beyond just classification?
questions.explanation: Human beings were interested not only in knowing more about different kinds of organisms and their diversities, but ALSO the relationships among them — this led to systematics.

questions.question: The specific epithet 'sapiens' in Homo sapiens belongs to which starting letter convention?
questions.explanation: The specific epithet always starts with a small letter. 'sapiens' in Homo sapiens starts with small 's'.

questions.question: Which unit/textbook does Chapter 1 'The Living World' belong to?

options.B: Unit 1 — Diversity in the Living World

questions.explanation: Chapter 1 'The Living World' belongs to Unit 1 — 'Diversity in the Living World' which also includes Chapters 2, 3 and 4.

questions.question: Unit 1 of NCERT Class 11 Biology contains which chapters?
questions.explanation: Unit 1 (Diversity in the Living World) contains Chapter 1 (The Living World), Chapter 2 (Biological Classification), Chapter 3 (Plant Kingdom) and Chapter 4 (Animal Kingdom).

questions.question: Which of the following describes the relationship between housefly (Musca domestica) and man (Homo sapiens)?

options.A: They belong to the same kingdom but different phyla
options.B: They belong to the same phylum
options.C: They belong to the same class
options.D: They belong to the same order

questions.explanation: Man and housefly both belong to Kingdom Animalia but different phyla — man belongs to Chordata and housefly to Arthropoda.

questions.question: What is the phylum of man?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to phylum Chordata.

questions.question: The sections in Chapter 1 (The Living World) as per NCERT are:

options.B: 1.1 Diversity in the Living World and 1.2 Taxonomic Categories

questions.explanation: NCERT Chapter 1 (The Living World) has sections: 1.1 Diversity in the Living World and 1.2 Taxonomic Categories.

questions.question: The sub-sections of Section 1.2 (Taxonomic Categories) include categories from species to kingdom. Which sub-section deals with Phylum?
questions.explanation: The sub-sections are 1.2.1 (Species), 1.2.2 (Genus), 1.2.3 (Family), 1.2.4 (Order), 1.2.5 (Class), 1.2.6 (Phylum), 1.2.7 (Kingdom).

questions.question: Which sub-section of NCERT Chapter 1 deals with Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Sub-section 1.2.7 deals with Kingdom as the highest taxonomic category.

questions.question: The Chapter 1 exercise question 7 asks for the correct sequence of taxonomical categories. Which option is CORRECT?
questions.explanation: From NCERT exercise Q7, option (c) Species → Genus → Order → Phylum is the correct sequence.

questions.question: Which of the sequences from the NCERT exercise Q7 is INCORRECT?

options.C: All three sequences given are wrong

questions.explanation: Species → Order → Phylum → Kingdom is incorrect because Genus and Family come between Species and Order, and Class comes between Order and Phylum.

questions.question: From which perspective does Unit 1 of NCERT provide a description of animals and plants?
questions.explanation: NCERT states: 'you will get a description, including classification, of animals and plants from a taxonomist's perspective.'

questions.question: The Chapter 1 summary mentions that the basics of taxonomy like identification, naming and classification of organisms are universally evolved under:
questions.explanation: The basics of taxonomy like identification, naming and classification of organisms are universally evolved under international codes (ICBN and ICZN).

questions.question: The summary of Chapter 1 states that taxonomic studies are useful for 'knowing our bio-resources and their':
questions.explanation: Taxonomic studies are useful in general for knowing our bio-resources and their diversity.

questions.question: The Chapter 1 summary states that each organism is identified and assigned a correct scientific/biological name comprising ______ words as per the binomial system:
questions.explanation: Each organism is identified and assigned a correct scientific/biological name comprising two words as per the binomial system of nomenclature.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms from NCERT Table 1.1 belongs to order Diptera?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to order Diptera as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms from NCERT Table 1.1 belongs to order Primata?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to order Primata as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms from NCERT Table 1.1 belongs to order Poales?
questions.explanation: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to order Poales as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms from NCERT Table 1.1 belongs to class Mammalia?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to class Mammalia as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: In NCERT Table 1.1, which organism belongs to class Dicotyledonae?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to class Dicotyledonae as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: In NCERT Table 1.1, which organism belongs to class Monocotyledonae?
questions.explanation: Wheat (Triticum aestivum) belongs to class Monocotyledonae as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: In NCERT Table 1.1, which organism belongs to family Muscidae?
questions.explanation: Housefly (Musca domestica) belongs to family Muscidae as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: In NCERT Table 1.1, which organism belongs to family Hominidae?
questions.explanation: Man (Homo sapiens) belongs to family Hominidae as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: In NCERT Table 1.1, which organism belongs to family Anacardiaceae?
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) belongs to family Anacardiaceae as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: In NCERT Table 1.1, which division does both mango and wheat belong to?
questions.explanation: Both mango (Mangifera indica) and wheat (Triticum aestivum) belong to division Angiospermae as given in NCERT Table 1.1.

questions.question: Which of the following is a HIGHER taxon than Order?
questions.explanation: In the hierarchy, Class comes above Order. Species, Genus and Family are all BELOW Order.

questions.question: Which of the following is a LOWER taxon than Order?
questions.explanation: In the hierarchy, Family comes below Order. Class, Phylum and Kingdom are all ABOVE Order.

questions.question: Which of the following is between Genus and Order in the taxonomic hierarchy?
questions.explanation: The hierarchy is: Genus → Family → Order. Family comes between Genus and Order.

questions.question: Which of the following is between Order and Kingdom in the taxonomic hierarchy?
questions.explanation: The hierarchy is: Order → Class → Phylum/Division → Kingdom. Class and Phylum/Division come between Order and Kingdom.

questions.question: According to NCERT, the problem of classification becomes more complex when:

options.A: We study lower taxa like species
options.B: We go to higher categories like Kingdom
options.C: We study organisms with similar morphology
options.D: We study organisms in the same genus

questions.explanation: Higher the category, greater is the difficulty of determining the relationship to other taxa at the same level. Hence, the problem of classification becomes more complex at higher levels.

questions.question: Which of the following examples from NCERT demonstrates that early man had interest in organisms?

options.A: Early man classified organisms for science
options.B: Early man deified animals and plants and had interest in organisms for basic needs
options.C: Early man had no interest in organisms

questions.explanation: Early man deified some animals and plants, and needed to find sources for basic needs — both showing early interest in organisms.

questions.question: The genus 'Homo' differs from genus 'Panthera' in that:

options.A: Homo includes cats and Panthera includes humans
options.B: They belong to different families, orders and classes
options.C: Homo contains species sapiens (humans) and Panthera contains big cats like lion, tiger, leopard

questions.explanation: Homo is the genus for humans (Homo sapiens) while Panthera is the genus for lion (P. leo), tiger (P. tigris) and leopard (P. pardus).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a section title in NCERT Chapter 1 'The Living World'?
questions.explanation: Chapter 1 has sections: 1.1 Diversity in the Living World and 1.2 Taxonomic Categories. 'Biological Classification' is Chapter 2.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about the sub-section 1.2.3 (Family) in NCERT?

options.B: It discusses Family as a category having a group of related genera characterised on vegetative and reproductive features

questions.explanation: Section 1.2.3 discusses Family — a category with a group of related genera, characterised on the basis of both vegetative and reproductive features of plant species.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about section 1.2.4 (Order) in NCERT?

options.C: Order is an assemblage of families exhibiting a few similar characters

questions.explanation: Section 1.2.4 states: Order being a higher category, is the assemblage of families which exhibit a few similar characters.

questions.question: What type of organisms does the living world comprise according to the opening of Chapter 1?

options.C: An amazing diversity of living organisms

questions.explanation: The opening of Chapter 1 states: 'The living world comprises an amazing diversity of living organisms.'

questions.question: NCERT mentions that in plants, classes with a few similar characters are assigned to a higher category called:
questions.explanation: In case of plants, classes with a few similar characters are assigned to a higher category called Division (the equivalent of Phylum for animals).

questions.question: A 'genus' can have how many specific epithets?

options.D: Unlimited but must be from the same kingdom

questions.explanation: Each genus may have one or more than one specific epithets representing different organisms, but having morphological similarities.

questions.question: The opening statement of NCERT Chapter 1 says 'How wonderful is the living world!' This reflects which approach to studying biology?

options.B: Sense of awe and wonder towards living organisms

questions.explanation: The opening reflects a sense of wonder and awe towards the living world — the beauty of a galloping horse, migrating birds, valley of flowers or an attacking shark.

questions.question: According to NCERT, which information is essential for modern taxonomic studies?

options.B: External and internal structure, cell structure, development process and ecological information

questions.explanation: External and internal structure, along with the structure of cell, development process and ecological information of organisms are essential and form the basis of modern taxonomic studies.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why scientific nomenclature is 'acceptable to biologists all over the world'?

options.A: Because it is based on local languages
options.B: Because it is based on universal codes (ICBN, ICZN) and Latin language, making it consistent worldwide
options.C: Because it was mandated by the United Nations

questions.explanation: Scientists have established procedures based on universal codes (ICBN for plants, ICZN for animals) using Latin — making scientific names acceptable to biologists all over the world.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct statement about 'Class' as a taxonomic category?
questions.explanation: Class includes related orders. For example, orders Primata and Carnivora are both placed in class Mammalia.

questions.question: The taxonomic categories in NCERT Table 1.1 are listed as: Common Name, Biological Name, Genus, Family, Order, Class, and Phylum/Division. Which column is missing from this list?
questions.explanation: NCERT Table 1.1 shows: Common Name, Biological Name (which is genus+species), Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum/Division — Kingdom is not separately listed as a column.

questions.question: In NCERT Chapter 1, the example of 'Alsatian' is used to illustrate what?

options.A: That some organisms have no scientific name
options.B: That specific classification can give precise identity to organisms
options.C: That all dogs look the same
options.D: That Alsatians are a different species from dogs

questions.explanation: NCERT uses the Alsatian example to show that specific classification/naming gives a precise identity — 'if we think of Alsatians, we know exactly what we are talking about.'

questions.question: Which of the following is the MAIN purpose of biological classification according to NCERT?

options.A: To count the number of organisms
options.B: To facilitate the study of kinds and diversity of organisms
options.C: To assign economic values to organisms
options.D: To determine which organisms are useful to humans

questions.explanation: Biologists have evolved certain rules and principles for identification, nomenclature and classification of organisms in order to facilitate the study of kinds and diversity of organisms.

questions.question: Which of the following is the biological name (binomial) of lion?
questions.explanation: The biological name (binomial) of lion is Panthera leo — Panthera (genus) and leo (specific epithet).

questions.question: Which of the following is the biological name (binomial) of housefly?
questions.explanation: The biological name (binomial) of housefly is Musca domestica.

questions.question: Which of the following is the biological name (binomial) of human?
questions.explanation: The biological name (binomial) of human is Homo sapiens.

questions.question: Which of the following is the biological name (binomial) of mango?
questions.explanation: The biological name (binomial) of mango is Mangifera indica.

questions.question: Which of the following is the biological name (binomial) of wheat?
questions.explanation: The biological name (binomial) of wheat is Triticum aestivum.

questions.question: Chapter 1 'The Living World' NCERT concludes with how many exercise questions?
questions.explanation: NCERT Chapter 1 (The Living World) ends with 10 exercise questions (Q1 to Q10).
